title: WHO is Accountable Act
stage_label: Referred to the House Committee on Foreign Affairs.
plain_summary: This bill stops the United States from giving money to the World Health Organization or trying to be a member until the WHO makes certain changes. The WHO would need to promise not to let politics affect their health work and not be controlled by China's government. They would also need to let Taiwan participate as an observer and stop working on topics like climate change and abortion. The State Department would have to tell Congress that all these conditions are met before the US could rejoin or pay the WHO. The bill is meant to make the WHO focus only on health issues and be more independent.
last_action_text: Referred to the House Committee on Foreign Affairs.

sections.title: Section 1 Short Title
sections.text: This law can be called the WHO is Accountable Act.

sections.title: Section 2 Prohibition on Use of Funds to Seek Membership in the World Health Organization or to Provide Assessed or Voluntary Contributions to the World Health Organization
sections.text: The United States government cannot use any money to join the World Health Organization or pay dues to the World Health Organization. This ban stays in place until the Secretary of State tells Congress that the World Health Organization has met all the required conditions. The required conditions are these eight things. First, the World Health Organization must make real changes to make sure that help for people in need is not used for political purposes and goes to those who need it most. Second, the World Health Organization cannot be controlled by or heavily influenced by the Chinese Communist Party. Third, the World Health Organization cannot be involved in hiding what the Chinese Communist Party did during the COVID nineteen pandemic. Fourth, the World Health Organization must give Taiwan observer status, which means Taiwan can attend meetings but not vote. Fifth, the World Health Organization cannot send medical supplies or aid to Iran, North Korea, or Syria. Sixth, the World Health Organization must create ways to be more open about what it does, be held responsible for its actions, and stop wasting money or allowing theft and fraud. Seventh, the World Health Organization must stop all funding, involvement, and messaging about certain controversial political issues that are not related to health work, including topics about gender identity and gender affirming care, climate change, and access to abortion. Eighth, the World Health Organization must agree that if the United States joins, any rules or orders the World Health Organization makes cannot be legally required for any American citizen or any individual state to follow.
